message:
🌱 Add envoy: Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster)  mission (#1715)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "envoy-3431-client-side-alpn-support-upstream-cluster",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "envoy: Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) ",
+    "description": "Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) . Requested by 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current envoy deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your envoy version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nhelm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working envoy installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review envoy configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nkubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy\n```\n*Title*: *Envoy should support client side protocol negotiation via alpn*\n\n*Description*:\nAt present if the an upstream cluster has `http2_protocolOptions`, it will always send h2 traffic upstream."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) ",
+        "description": "Optimally, this would be done as part of also solving https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/1502. The right solution here is to have a new connection pool implementation that can do both HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, ALPN negotiation, upgrade, etc. I would prefer to not complicate the existing connection pool implementations with this logic.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade envoy to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade envoy:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade envoy envoy/envoy --namespace envoy\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n envoy\nhelm list -n envoy\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy\nkubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Client side ALPN support (upstream cluster) \" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Optimally, this would be done as part of also solving https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/1502. The right solution here is to have a new connection pool implementation that can do both HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, ALPN negotiation, upgrade, etc. I would prefer to not complicate the existing connection pool implementations with this logic.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "envoy",
+      "graduated",
+      "networking",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],

+    },

+  },

+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",

+      "kubectl"
+    ],

+  },
